message: fix(core): reject reserved dot path components

 {"id":"bd-o9bzg","title":"add ffs-core fuzz target for btrfs inode-ref payload parser","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs::btrfs_parse_inode_ref_payload(payload: &[u8]). Scope: add a narrow high-throughput libFuzzer target over raw payload bytes, seeded with valid inode-ref encodings and truncated/overflowing name-length variants. Assert parser totality (no panics), bounded output growth, and round-trip compatibility with the serializer on successfully parsed cases where names are valid. Acceptance: new fuzz target registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml, corpus seeds/minimized regressions checked in, and parser invariants documented in the target comments or coverage notes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:49:24.704304257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:26:07.549378387Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:26:07.547205058Z","close_reason":"Already implemented under fuzz/fuzz_targets/ (detect_filesystem/verify_ext4_integrity/btrfs_inode_ref_payload/path_component_validation).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fuzz","parser","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-o9l8s","title":"port ext4 large_dir htree depth parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T12:26:00.106283757Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T12:32:16.515950270Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T12:32:16.513756695Z","close_reason":"Implemented ext4 large_dir htree depth parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-oa8g2","title":"Reject reserved dot path components in filesystem writes","description":"Reviewer finding [HIGH]: ffs-core accepts reserved path components "." and ".." as user-supplied directory entry names in mutating filesystem operations.

Evidence:
- crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:14500 validate_single_path_component only rejects empty names, embedded slash, and names longer than 255 bytes. It returns Ok for b"." and b"..".
- The validator feeds ext4 create/mknod/link/rename/unlink paths and btrfs create/mkdir/link/symlink/rename/unlink paths before directory mutation. For example, btrfs_create validates at crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:13207 and then inserts the provided name into the directory index; btrfs_link validates at 13586 and later stores new_name in a BtrfsDirItem.
- fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_path_component_validation.rs models only Empty, ContainsSlash, TooLong, and Valid. Its oracle treats "." and ".." as Valid, so the existing fuzz target cannot catch the reserved-component bug.

Why this matters:
"." and ".." are reserved namespace entries. Accepting them for create/mkdir/link/symlink/rename can shadow or conflict with synthesized parent/current-directory semantics and can corrupt lookup/readdir/link-count behavior.

Expected behavior:
Reject b"." and b".." with a format/invalid-name error before allocating inodes or mutating directory entries, across ext4 and btrfs create, mkdir, mknod, link, symlink, unlink/rmdir as applicable, and rename old/new components.

Reproduction direction:
Add regression tests that attempt create/link/symlink/rename using "." and ".." under both ext4 and btrfs test filesystems. Assert the operation fails and pre-existing source/directory state remains unchanged.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T12:06:59.648114127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T19:48:24.224159584Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T19:48:24.222193278Z","close_reason":"Completed: central path-component validator rejects reserved dot names; ext4/btrfs mutation regressions and fuzz oracle updated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-core","path-validation","review"]}
 {"id":"bd-oazg","title":"Add btrfs item payload adversarial parser coverage","description":"Fallback testing-fuzzing parity round: add deterministic adversarial regression coverage for ffs-btrfs item payload parsers: parse_root_item, parse_inode_item, parse_dir_items, parse_extent_data, and adjacent xattr parsing if practical. Cover valid payloads, truncation, length overflows, unknown/invalid type fields, and multi-entry boundaries; update conformance/COVERAGE.md.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T07:22:42.612231865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T07:38:44.452894598Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T07:38:44.450681129Z","close_reason":"Completed btrfs item payload parser adversarial regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","fuzz","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-obfn","title":"Hardening epic: dynamic constants, CRC correctness, and unfinished subsystems","description":"Umbrella epic for all findings from the comprehensive hard-coded constant audit, fresh-eyes code review, and unfinished subsystem identification performed on 2026-03-25. Covers six categories: (1) bitmap CRC32C mismatch against real mkfs images, (2) i_version increment-on-write for NFS, (3) e2compr indirect block write support, (4) external journal paired replay, (5) fast commit operations apply, (6) stale docs/spec text. Each category has its own child bead with detailed acceptance criteria.  {"id":"bd-oflc","title":"Fix empty-bitmap contiguous search panic in ffs-alloc","description":"## Purpose

`bitmap_find_contiguous()`/`bitmap_find_contiguous_linear()` should treat out-of-range bits as allocated and return `None`, not index past the end of an empty bitmap.

## Scope

1. Reproduce the failing property test / panic surface for empty bitmaps with non-zero count.
2. Fix the bitmap scan logic so empty or undersized bitmaps are handled safely.
3. Keep allocator semantics unchanged for in-range bits.
4. Add/keep regression coverage for empty bitmap contiguous search.

## Acceptance criteria
- No panic on empty bitmap with non-zero count
- `bitmap_find_contiguous(&[], count, 1, start) == None`
- Targeted `ffs-alloc` tests pass via `rch`
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-20T03:31:37.922100179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T03:36:42.994690280Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T03:36:42.994159476Z","close_reason":"Already fixed by bd-xh0u and verified by existing proptest_empty_bitmap_nonzero_count_has_no_free.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alloc","safety","testing"]}
